Here, 'some of what We promise them' (ba'dal-ladhee na'iduhum) points to the punishment of this world, because in terms of reality, it is a part of that punishment of the Hereafter which all disbelievers and polytheists must face in the Hereafter. The Prophet's (pbuh) nation was warned of punishment in this world in case of denial, but the majority of his nation accepted Islam; because of this, no such punishment came upon them as came upon previous nations, but the accounting of its rebels was delayed for the Hereafter, which is pointed to with 'to Us they will be returned' (ilaina yurja'oon).

A Specific Aspect of Consolation

This explains the theme of consolation from a specific aspect. The deniers of the messengers continuously demanded the punishment to harass the messenger. One reply was given in the preceding verse, and another aspect is explained here—showing that the history of the messengers is witness that showing such signs is not in the control of the messenger, but relates entirely to the wisdom of Allah Almighty. If His wisdom requires, He sends the punishment; and if it does not, He does not send it. People should not demand this of the messenger, nor should the messenger be worried by their demand. However, it must be remembered well that when Allah's command for the punishment is issued, decisions are made with complete justice, and at that time, ruin falls upon those who deny the truth and the messengers.

Reference to the Signs of Lordship

Above, the demand for the sign of punishment was pointed to. The Prophet (pbuh) was assured that this relates to Allah Almighty; therefore, the matter must be referred to Him. In this verse, those who demand the sign of punishment are directed to the signs of His lordship—implying: why do you demand the sign of punishment alone, why do you not reflect on the countless signs of His lordship around you! He created cattle for you, some of which you use for riding, and some of which you use for food, and besides this, many other benefits are associated with them. The Creator has made them such that you ride upon them to embark on your journeys and fulfill your plans! This points to the camel, which held the position of the 'ship of the desert' for the Arabs. It states: 'You ride upon them in your long desert journeys, and similarly travel in ships in the sea! Are all these not the signs of God! Whichever of His signs will you deny!' The meaning is: reflect that the Sustainer Who has made this arrangement for your needs, will He leave you without purpose, will He not question you regarding these blessings! If the answer is in the affirmative, then His warning is what the Prophet is warning you of. Then, what is this excuse you have devised for his denial—claiming that he is not showing you any sign of punishment! There is no shortage of signs, but you do not possess eyes to see them.

This directs those who demand the punishment to the history of previous nations—showing: if they do not see the signs of His lordship, have they never traveled through the land to see what was the end of those before them who denied the messengers? Although they were greater than them in power and in terms of their cultural and architectural monuments, yet when God's punishment came, those things which they had accumulated were of no use to them.

'Athar' (monuments) relates to 'akthar' (more abundant). This points to those architectural monuments of 'Aad and Thamud whose mention has passed. This shows that when the nation falls into moral decline, it cannot survive merely on the basis of its numbers and cultural monuments.

It states: when Allah's messengers came to these nations with highly clear proofs of Tawhid, the Hereafter, and reward and punishment, they mocked their words and remained proud of their own knowledge. Ultimately, they were enveloped by that punishment which they mocked. Meaning, their limited knowledge they considered a highly great thing due to their narrow-mindedness, and assumed that whatever they see is everything. They could not understand that:

'There are other worlds beyond the stars.'

That is, they did not believe because of the messengers' proofs, but when they saw Allah's punishment, they cried out: 'We have believed in Allah alone, and we deny those partners we associated with Him!' It states: their faith which they brought after seeing the punishment was of no use to them; rather, the punishment destroyed them. Credible faith is that which is brought in the light of proofs before seeing the punishment. If that time passed and the hour of punishment arrived, the faith of that time is entirely useless. It states: this Sunnah of Allah has always been in effect among His servants. When the punishment comes, those who deny necessarily face ruin.

By the grace of Allah, the commentary of this Surah is completed on these lines. Praise be to Allah for this.

Lahore
27 August, 1975 AD
19 Shaban, 1395 AH.
